chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR C.P.No. D- 2379 of 2017 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge. Application in d/o case 1.For orders on CMA 1644/22 2.For orders on CMA 1646/22 3.For orders on CMA 1645/22 11-05-2022 Mr. Sajjad Muhammad Zangejo, Advocate for the Petitioner. ***** 1. Granted. 2. Granted subject to all just legal exceptions. 3. Through this Application, the Petitioner seeks review of Order dated 24.03.2022, whereby this Petition was dismissed. Learned Counsel for the Petitioner submits that some other similarly placed persons had filed Petition before the Principal seat at Karachi and consent was given therein on behalf of the Respondents and the Petition was allowed. Hence, the Petitioner is also entitled for the same relief and therefore the Order in question be reviewed. We have heard the Petitioner’s Counsel and perused the record. Insofar as the present Petition is concerned, there is no consent on behalf of the Respondents; whereas, it has been stated that the Petitioner had failed in the interview test. In the similar circumstances by following the Judgment in the case reported as Muhammad Ashraf Sangri v. Federation of Pakistan (2014 SCMR 157) we have already dismissed various Petitions including the present Petition. No case for indulgence is made out; therefore, instant Review Application stands dismissed. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
